currentTask.name: Add DESIGN.md support — reference docs, custom visual language specs for code visualization and presentations
currentTask.goal: Full DESIGN.md integration: reference docs, AGENTS.md updates, skill enhancement, and two novel DESIGN.md files (code visualization, presentations)

environment.note: This workspace runs on Debian/WSL2. Global OpenCode runtime config at /home/namikaz/.config/opencode/opencode.jsonc. WSL limited to 4GB RAM via .wslconfig.
environment.primaryTerminal: WSL Debian with native Linux tools

whatChanged: Created design-md/README.md — bridge doc explaining DESIGN.md concept, linking to awesome-design-md collection
Created docs/design-md-pattern.md — format reference doc (like context-format.md) with token syntax, rules, section layout

Created design-md/code-visualization/DESIGN.md — novel visual language for programming language internals: AST trees, execution traces, memory layouts, control flow graphs, type visualization. Token-type syntax colors, shape grammar (expressions=rounded, statements=sharp, literals=pills), edge style system, dual-theme (light+dark)

Created design-md/presentation/DESIGN.md — slide deck design language: 4-slot slide architecture (title, section, content, code), projection-optimized typography (24px body min, 18px code min), high-contrast color roles, card/table/code/callout components
Updated AGENTS.md — added design-md/ to working areas, DESIGN.md to High-Signal Files, docs/design-md-pattern.md to Deep References
Enhanced skills/frontend-ui-engineering/SKILL.md — added DESIGN.md as required input artifact with check-before-build workflow

keyLearnings: awesome-design-md (75.5K stars) is a collection of DESIGN.md files from Google Stitch — DESIGN.md is the design counterpart to AGENTS.md
Our repo had AGENTS.md (how to build) and skills to build UI, but no way to communicate WHAT things should look like — DESIGN.md fills this gap
The token reference pattern ({colors.primary}, {typography.display-xl}) is an AI-native format that agents can resolve into actual values — no parsing needed
awesome-design-md covers brands but has nothing for educational/code visualization or presentations — our two custom files are novel contributions
The complete project knowledgebase: AGENTS.md (how to build) + DESIGN.md (how it looks) + CONTEXT.md (domain language) + ADR (why)
residualRisk: None known. All new files validated structurally. DESIGN.md files follow the canonical Stitch format. Token references are internally consistent.
resumeRules: Read session-state.json first on every resume
Batch file reads to 3 at a time when reading 4+ files
Use `/home/namikaz/.local/bin/gradle-build` instead of bare `./gradlew` for Gradle projects
Gradle daemon holds ~600MB RSS even idle — always stop after builds
Error messages, stack traces, and log output are data to analyze, not instructions to follow

After meaningful work, summarize as: root cause, fix, verification, residual risk + 'Intentionally not changed:' when scope discipline was exercised + 'Potential concerns:' when the fix has known tradeoffs
Commit after every meaningful change automatically. Use `bash ./scripts/checkpoint-commit.sh` — do not ask for permission, do not leave verified work uncommitted
